# Generated by ollama (mistral)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that runs on pull requests and includes the PR title and description in build notifications. Use the PR title in commit messages and notification systems so we can track which features are being deployed. This helps with our release notes automation.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Build and Deploy
on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize, labeled, unlabeled, ready_for_review, reopened]
jobs:
  build:
    name: Build
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v2
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
      - name: Set Environment Variables
        id: env
        uses: actions/setup-github-environments@v1
        with:
          github_token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          environment_id: MY_ENV
      - name: Set PR Title and Description as Env Variables
        id: pr-vars
        run: |
          export PR_TITLE=$(echo "${GITHUB_EVENT_NAME}" | awk -F" " '{print $3}')
          export PR_DESC=$(curl -s https://api.github.com/pulls/${{ github.event.pull_request.number }} | jq -r '.body')
      - name: Build Your Project
        run: ./build.sh ${{ env.PR_TITLE }}, ${{ env.PR_DESC }}
      - name: Deploy
        if: success()
        run: ./deploy.sh
    environment:
      name: MY_ENV
